rule_text: Candlestick Reversal/Continuation Pattern: A reversal candle should cause price to drop (close below previous low = success, close above preceding candle top = failure). For continuation candle: identification of candle in established direction

rule_text: Unusually Tall Candle: A candlestick that is taller than average, occurring above the highs of two and three days prior (uptrend) or below the lows of two and three days prior (downtrend), with height exceeding 146% of the average high-low range of the prior 22 trading days.
entry_conditions: The tall candle must be above the highs of two and three days ago (for uptrends) or below the lows of two and three days ago (for downtrends)
Calculate the average high-low height of the prior 22 trading days (a calendar month), not including the current candle
Multiply the average height by 146%
The current candle height must be above the result to qualify as an unusually tall candle
exit_conditions: Expect a peak within a day from unusually tall candles (67% of the time during uptrend) or a valley within a day (72% of the time in downtrend)

rule_text: Pattern name: Eight New Price Lines (bullish candle pattern). Price climbs to a new high each day with most candles white, demonstrating rampant bullish enthusiasm. rule_text: Trend Change Identification: Three-step method to confirm a trend has reversed from downtrend to uptrend
entry_conditions: Draw a trendline from the highest high (A) along subsequent highs toward the lowest low (B, always after the highest high) such that price does not intersect the trendline until after the lowest low
Price closes above the trendline (occurs at point 1) - first indication of trend change
Price tests the low by attempting to make a new low but fails (point 2) - it is acceptable if price moves slightly below the recent low and then rebounds, but must fail to hold lower
Price recovers to close above the high between the lowest low and the subsequent high

rule_text: Trend Change Pattern: Price recovery above the high between the lowest low and the test of that low signals a trend change from down to up
entry_conditions: Identify the lowest low at point B
Identify the test of that low at point 2
Identify the high between the lowest low and the test at point C
Entry signal occurs when price closes above point C at point 3
Close above C fulfills the three steps of a trend change

rule_text: Concealing Baby Swallow: A four-candleline pattern consisting of two long black candles without shadows, followed by a black candle with upper shadow that gaps down but trades into prior day's body, completed by a black candle that engulfs the prior day including shadows. Last day: Another black day that completely engulfs the prior day, including the shadows
Total pattern length: Four candleline days

rule_text: Deliberation candle pattern: Begins in uptrend with two long white candles showing bullish momentum, followed by a third day with a small body (possibly with tall shadows) indicating confusion and loss of bullish momentum
entry_conditions: Pattern begins in an uptrend
First two days form long white candles showing bullish buying pressure
Third day forms a candle with a small body despite possible tall shadows
Small body on day three emphasizes confusion and indecision over direction
Small body represents loss of bullish momentum compared to prior two days
Third day can be small white body (bullish) or black body (bearish)
Day after pattern ends is key for signal confirmation
exit_conditions: Day after pattern ends determines directional outcome

rule_text: Phillip's Candle: Use candle height as a percentage of breakout price to calculate price targets in both upward and downward directions
entry_conditions: Identify a candle formation (Phillip's candle)
Determine breakout price
Measure candle height as percentage of breakout price
exit_conditions: Upward price target: Breakout price + (candle height × percentage meeting price target)
Downward price target: Breakout price − (candle height × percentage meeting price target)
Example: For price 39 with 88% upward target: 39 + (1 × 88%) = 39.88
Example: For price 38 with 82% downward target: 38 − (1 × 82%) = 37.18

rule_text: Candle Pattern: Tall candles show better performance than short candles. Height is determined by computing the range from highest high to lowest low price divided by the breakout price, compared against the median.
entry_conditions: Compute candle height: (highest high - lowest low) / breakout price
Compare result to median height value
If result is higher than median: TALL candle (favorable)
If result is lower than median: SHORT candle (less favorable)
For up breakouts: candles near yearly high perform best
For down breakouts: candles within a third of yearly low perform best

rule_text: Hanging Man pattern: A candle forming after an uptrend with a trendline matching the top of a marubozu, followed by three days of higher lows and lower highs (squeezing movement), culminating in a hanging man candle that signals trend reversal
entry_conditions: Uptrend must be in place
Trendline matches the top of a marubozu candle
Three days of higher lows and lower highs follow (squeezing movement)
Final candle must be a hanging man candle
Sell signal occurs at the open of the next day after hanging man formation

rule_text: Mat Hold: A five-candle bullish continuation pattern where price eases upward after completion, consisting of a tall white candle followed by a small black candle gap-up, two small-bodied candles, and a white candle close above prior four candles' highs.
entry_conditions: Upward price trend leading to the start of the candle pattern
First day: A long white body candle
Second day: Price gaps open upward but closes lower (small black candle), remains above the prior close, ignore shadows
Days 3 and 4: Day 3 can be any color, day 4 is black. Both candles have small bodies with closing price easing lower, but bodies remain above the low of the first day
Last day: A white candle with a close above the highs of the prior four candles

rule_text: Pattern name: Rising Three Methods. Five candle lines consisting of a tall white candle, followed by three small bodies (usually black, middle one can be black or white), followed by another white candle. Second candle gaps above the first and closes higher. Middle three candles trend lower with bodies remaining above the first candle's low.
entry_conditions: First candle is a tall white candle
Second candle gaps above the first candle and closes higher than the first candle
Three small bodies follow (usually all black, but middle one can be either black or white)
All three middle bodies should trend lower
All three middle bodies should remain above the first candle's low
Fifth candle is a white candle

rule_text: Mat Hold pattern: Candle height measurement and target price calculation using measure rule
entry_conditions: Identify mat hold candlestick pattern
Calculate candle height from highest high to lowest low
Determine if candle is tall or short by dividing height by breakout price and comparing to median
In bull market: if result higher than median = tall candle; otherwise = short candle
exit_conditions: Measure rule: Compute candle pattern height and multiply by appropriate percentage from table (example: 42% for upward breakout); apply result to breakout price to calculate target

rule_text: Candlestick pattern shadow analysis: upper shadow length affects post-breakout performance based on market direction and breakout direction
entry_conditions: Identify the last candleline in the pattern
Compute shadow height and divide by breakout price
Compare result to median shadow value in reference table
Tall shadows have percentage higher than the median
Short shadows have percentage lower than or equal to median
exit_conditions: For bear market/down breakout category with unmet targets, use 67% of pattern heights
target_regimes: Bull market with up breakout - tall shadows perform better
Bear market with down breakout - tall shadows perform better
Bear market with up breakout - short shadows perform better
Bull market with down breakout - short shadows perform better
